message:
feat(artifact-merge): add semantic diff between artifact versions

Implements diffArtifacts(v1, v2) to show WHAT changed between artifact
versions - not just whether valid. Includes ArtifactDiff type,
formatDiffHuman and formatDiffJson formatters, and progress heuristic.

Features:
- Tracks added/killed/edited items per section
- Hypothesis refinement detection (kill + add = active refinement)
- Critique resolution tracking
- Anomaly promotion/dismissal detection
- Progress score (NONE/MINIMAL/MODERATE/GOOD/EXCELLENT)

Closes brenner_bot-30st

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-2qyl","title":"Feature: Research Program Dashboard","description":"# Feature: Research Program Dashboard

## Purpose
Aggregate research progress across multiple sessions into a coherent program view. Enable researchers to understand the overall state of their research without reading every session artifact.

## Background
The Brenner method naturally spans multiple sessions. Currently, there is no way to get a high-level view. This dashboard provides that view.

## Research Program Concept

A **Research Program** groups related sessions:

```typescript
interface ResearchProgram {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  sessions: string[];
  createdAt: string;
  status: "active" | "paused" | "completed" | "abandoned";
  notes?: string;
}
```

## Dashboard Components

### 1. Hypothesis Funnel
Visual representation of hypothesis lifecycle with breakdown by origin:
```
Proposed: 12 (8 original, 3 third-alternative, 1 anomaly-spawned)
    ↓
Active: 5  |  Under Attack: 2  |  Assumption-Undermined: 1
    ↓
Killed: 6  |  Validated: 0  |  Dormant: 1
```

### 2. Registry Health Metrics

**Hypothesis Registry:**
- Total hypotheses: Count by state
- Kill rate: Killed / (Killed + Active + Validated)
- Third alternative coverage: % of hypothesis sets with third alt

**Assumption Registry:**
- Unchecked assumptions: Count (warning if > 5)
- Scale/physics checks: Count (warning if 0!)
- Falsified assumptions: Count + impact (which hypotheses affected)

**Anomaly Registry:**
- Active anomalies: Count (unresolved puzzles)
- Deferred anomalies: Count (parked questions)
- Paradigm-shifting: Count (if > 0, major finding!)
- Resolution rate: Resolved / Total

**Critique Registry:**
- Active critiques: Count (unaddressed attacks)
- Critiques by severity: minor/moderate/serious/critical breakdown
- Response rate: Addressed / Total
- Hypothesis under attack: List (high unaddressed count)

### 3. Test Execution Metrics
```
Designed: 8  |  Executed: 3  |  Pending: 5
Potency checks: 3/8 (37%) ⚠️
Average evidence-per-week score: 7.2/12
```

### 4. Active Hypotheses
List of hypotheses in "active" state with threat level:
```
H-RS20251230-001: Cell fate is epigenetically determined [active, 2 critiques]
H-RS20251231-003: Third alternative: chromatin accessibility [active, clean]
```

### 5. Blocked Tests
Tests that are designed but not yet executed:
```
T-RS20251230-002: Lineage tracing with Cre-lox [designed, 3 days, missing potency!]
T-RS20251231-001: Single-cell ATAC-seq [designed, 1 day]
```

### 6. Unresolved Anomalies
Active anomalies that need attention:
```
X-RS20251230-001: AB lineage shows position-dependent fates [active, challenges H1, H2]
```

### 7. Assumption Warnings
Scale checks and falsified assumptions:
```
⚠️ No scale/physics assumptions recorded! Add at least one.
A-RS20251230-003: Diffusion timescale check [verified, τ ≈ 17 min]
A-RS20251231-001: Cell cycle length assumption [falsified → H2 undermined]
```

### 8. Key Findings
Auto-extracted from session artifacts:
- Killed hypotheses with reasoning
- Validated hypotheses (rare!)
- Falsified assumptions with cascade effects
- Resolved anomalies

### 9. Timeline
Chronological view of major events:
```
2025-12-30: Session RS-20251230 - 3 hypotheses proposed
2025-12-30: A1 (scale check) verified - gradient timing plausible
2025-12-31: H1 killed by T1 (unexpected methylation pattern)
2025-12-31: X1 spawned H4 (third alternative from anomaly)
2026-01-02: C3 (critical) addressed - H2 modified
```

## CLI Commands

- `brenner program create --name="..." --description="..."`
- `brenner program add-session <program-id> <session-id>`
- `brenner program dashboard <program-id>` — Full dashboard
- `brenner program dashboard <program-id> --section=hypotheses|assumptions|anomalies|critiques|tests`
- `brenner program health <program-id>` — Quick health check (warnings only)
- `brenner program export <program-id> --format=md|json`

## Health Check Warnings

The dashboard should surface warnings when:
- No scale/physics assumptions recorded
- Unchecked assumptions > 5
- Active critiques (especially severity=critical) > 3
- Active anomalies > 3
- Potency check coverage < 80%
- Any hypothesis has > 2 unaddressed critiques

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Research programs can be created and managed
- [ ] Sessions can be grouped into programs
- [ ] All four registries feed dashboard metrics
- [ ] Health check warnings implemented
- [ ] CLI generates readable dashboard output
- [ ] Timeline includes all registry events

## Depends On
- Hypothesis Registry Schema
- Hypothesis Lifecycle States
- Test→Hypothesis Binding
- Assumption Registry and Lifecycle
- Anomaly Registry
- Critique Registry

 {"id":"brenner_bot-2vj","title":"Bug: DELTA subject mismatch breaks session status","description":"# Problem
`session start`/kickoff prompts instruct agents to respond with subjects like `DELTA[<role display name>]: ...` (e.g. `DELTA[Test Designer]: ...`).

`session status` derives role completion by parsing subjects and appears to only recognize `DELTA[<shorthand>]` where shorthand is one of: `gpt`, `codex`, `opus`, `claude`, `gemini` (etc). If agents follow kickoff instructions literally, deltas may not be counted and a thread can remain "incomplete" forever.

# Done when
- Kickoff instructions and status parsing agree on DELTA subject format.
- Status correctly counts deltas for each role in both CLI + web (tests added).

# Proposed fix
- Prefer canonical shorthand in kickoff instructions: `DELTA[gpt]`, `DELTA[opus]`, `DELTA[gemini]`.
- Make status parsing robust to role display names (accept a small synonym map) so older threads still work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:41:17.466555154-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:53:29.365353032-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:53:29.365353032-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed DELTA subject mismatch: kickoff now uses shorthand tags; threadStatus parses both shorthand and role display names; added tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2vj","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.3.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:41:17.492550294-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-2wh","title":"Harden lab mode gating: require Cloudflare Access headers or BRENNER_LAB_SECRET","description":"Prevent accidental public exposure if BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 but Cloudflare Access is misconfigured and no app-layer secret is set.\n\nAcceptance:\n- If BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 AND no CF Access headers AND no BRENNER_LAB_SECRET: /sessions/* fails closed (404) and server actions deny.\n- If CF Access headers present OR valid BRENNER_LAB_SECRET (header/cookie): /sessions/new works.\n","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:00:49.576692067-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:01:02.296363303-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T22:01:02.296363303-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented fail-closed gating: /sessions/* now requires Cloudflare Access headers or BRENNER_LAB_SECRET (header/cookie); updated auth helpers + middleware + docs; lint/build pass","labels":["security","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2wh","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.4.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:00:49.58236171-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2wh","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.7.3.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:00:49.58385341-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-30st","title":"Feature: Artifact Semantic Diff (Version Evolution Viewer)","description":"# Goal
Implement semantic diffing between artifact versions to show WHAT changed, not just WHETHER it is valid.

## The Gap This Fills

Other agents covered:
- Artifact linting (validates structure, reports errors/warnings)
- Artifact merge (combines deltas into artifacts)
- Results encoding (how experiment outputs become deltas)

**What nobody covered:** When you compile v2 of an artifact, you see a lint report saying "2 errors, 5 warnings" - but you do NOT see:
- "Added hypothesis H4 (Epigenetic microcode)"
- "Killed hypothesis H2 (Gradient reading) with rationale: contradicted by T3 results"
- "Refined test T1: added potency check"
- "New assumption A6 added by adversarial_critic"

The lint report tells you validity; the semantic diff tells you evolution.

## Why This Matters

1. **Research Progress Visibility** - At a glance, see what changed between rounds. Did agents make progress or just churn?

2. **Critique Evaluation** - After a critique round, did agents address the feedback? The diff shows what was actually changed.

3. **Operator Decision Support** - Should I publish v2? The diff helps operators understand if changes are improvements.

4. **Debugging Bad Sessions** - If a session went wrong, the diff history shows where things diverged.

5. **Learning from Good Sessions** - Study how successful research evolved: what got killed early, what survived, what emerged late.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Semantic Diff Data Structure

```typescript
interface ArtifactDiff {
  from_version: number;
  to_version: number;
  changes: {
    hypothesis_slate: {
      added: Array<{ id: string; name: string; by_agent: string }>;
      killed: Array<{ id: string; name: string; rationale: string; by_agent: string }>;
      edited: Array<{ id: string; field: string; old_value: string; new_value: string }>;
    };
    test_battery: {
      added: Array<{ id: string; name: string; targets: string[] }>;
      edited: Array<{ id: string; field: string; old_value: string; new_value: string }>;
    };
    assumption_ledger: {
      added: Array<{ id: string; assumption: string; by_agent: string }>;
      challenged: Array<{ id: string; challenger: string; challenge: string }>;
    };
    critiques: {
      added: Array<{ id: string; target: string; critique: string; by_agent: string }>;
      resolved: Array<{ id: string; resolution: string }>;
    };
    anomaly_quarantine: {
      added: Array<{ id: string; description: string }>;
      promoted: Array<{ id: string; promoted_to: string }>; // anomaly became hypothesis
      dismissed: Array<{ id: string; reason: string }>;
    };
  };
  summary: {
    hypotheses_net: number;  // added - killed
    tests_added: number;
    critiques_resolved: number;
    progress_score: number;  // heuristic: are we making progress?
  };
}
```

### 2. Diff Generation Algorithm

```
1. Parse both artifact versions into normalized structures
2. For each section (hypothesis_slate, test_battery, etc.):
   a. Index items by ID
   b. Compare: items in v2 not in v1 = added
   c. Compare: items in v1 not in v2 = killed/removed
   d. Compare: items in both with different values = edited
3. For KILL operations specifically, preserve rationale from delta
4. Calculate summary statistics
```

### 3. Display Formats

**CLI Output:**
```
=== Artifact Diff: v1 → v2 ===
  
HYPOTHESES
  + [H4] Epigenetic microcode (by: GreenCastle)
  ✗ [H2] Gradient reading - KILLED: "contradicted by T3 results" (by: RedForest)
  ~ [H1] Lineage counting - edited: mechanism field refined
  
TESTS  
  + [T5] Digital handle task (targets: H1, H4)
  ~ [T1] Scale boundary probe - edited: added potency check
  
ASSUMPTIONS
  + [A6] Cells have stable chromatin state inheritance (by: RedForest)
  
CRITIQUES
  ✓ [C1] Missing potency check - RESOLVED by T1 edit
  + [C4] H4 mechanism underspecified (by: BlueMountain)
  
SUMMARY: +1 hypothesis, +1 test, 1 critique resolved | Progress: MODERATE
```

**Web UI:**
- Side-by-side or inline diff view
- Expandable sections
- Color-coded: green=added, red=killed, yellow=edited
- Filter by section type or agent

### 4. Integration Points

- **After Compile:** Show diff from previous version in compile preview
- **Session Detail Page:** "Changes" tab showing diff history
- **CLI:** `brenner session diff <threadId> [v1] [v2]`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `diffArtifacts(v1, v2)` function in artifact-merge.ts or new diff module
- [ ] Diff includes all sections: hypotheses, tests, assumptions, critiques, anomalies
- [ ] KILL operations preserve rationale in diff output
- [ ] CLI command `brenner session diff` implemented
- [ ] Web session detail page shows diff between versions
- [ ] Summary statistics calculated (net changes, progress heuristic)

## Dependencies

- Requires multiple artifact versions (already exists via COMPILED messages)
- Independent of cross-session linking (brenner_bot-prmc)

## Non-Goals

- Not implementing AI-powered "semantic similarity" (e.g., detecting renamed hypotheses)
- Not building revision history UI (showing all versions) - just v1→v2 diff
- Not computing "quality" of changes (thats evaluation rubric territory)

## Example Use Case

Operator runs a critique round. Agents respond with deltas. Operator compiles v2.

Without semantic diff:
```
Compiled v2 (lint: 0 errors, 3 warnings)
```

With semantic diff:
```
Compiled v2 (lint: 0 errors, 3 warnings)

Changes from v1:
  - Killed H2 (Gradient reading) - agents agreed T3 refuted it
  - Added T5 to test the surviving hypotheses
  - Resolved 2/3 critiques from round 1
  - New critique: H4 mechanism still underspecified
  
Progress: GOOD - hypotheses narrowing, tests targeting survivors
```

The operator can now make an informed decision about publishing.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:00:40.866901891-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:43:36.70824586-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-30st","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:38:33.056561414-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

```
  ~ [T1] Scale boundary probe - edited: added potency check
  
ASSUMPTIONS
  + [A6] Cells have stable chromatin state inheritance (by: RedForest)
  
CRITIQUES
  ✓ [C1] Missing potency check - RESOLVED by T1 edit
  + [C4] H4 mechanism underspecified (by: BlueMountain)
  
SUMMARY: +1 hypothesis, +1 test, 1 critique resolved | Progress: MODERATE
```

- **Session Detail Page:** "Changes" tab showing diff history
- **CLI:** `brenner session diff <threadId> [v1] [v2]`

Fix by using a trusted base URL (env/Vercel URL) or strict allowlist for local dev + brennerbot.org.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T03:47:59.670448559-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T04:05:05.926007263-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T04:05:05.926007263-05:00","close_reason":"Removed Host header trust for /_corpus fallback fetch: corpus.ts + operator-library.ts now use trusted base URL (BRENNER_PUBLIC_BASE_URL, VERCEL_URL, dev localhost fallback, else https://brennerbot.org)."}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-3fy","title":"TanStack Query: Remove Manual Caching from corpusSearch.ts","description":"# Goal
Remove the manual caching implementation in corpusSearch.ts and replace with Query.

## Background
corpusSearch.ts currently has manual cache management:
```typescript
let cachedSections: Section[] | null = null;
let cacheTimestamp: number | null = null;
const CACHE_TTL = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 minutes

export async function searchCorpus(query: string) {
  // Check cache freshness
  if (cachedSections && cacheTimestamp && 
      Date.now() - cacheTimestamp < CACHE_TTL) {
    return searchInSections(cachedSections, query);
  }
  
  // Refresh cache
  cachedSections = await loadAllSections();
  cacheTimestamp = Date.now();
  return searchInSections(cachedSections, query);
}
```

This pattern has issues:
1. Cache is module-scoped (memory leak potential in serverless)
2. No way to invalidate
3. Duplicates Query's functionality
4. No loading/error states exposed

## Implementation

### 1. Create useCorpusSections hook
```typescript
export function useCorpusSections() {
  return useQuery({
    queryKey: ['corpus', 'sections', 'all'],
    queryFn: loadAllSections,
    staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,
  });
}
```

### 2. Create useCorpusSearch hook
```typescript
export function useCorpusSearch(query: string) {
  const { data: sections } = useCorpusSections();
  
  return useQuery({
    queryKey: ['corpus', 'search', query],
    queryFn: () => searchInSections(sections!, query),
    enabled: !!sections && query.length >= 2,
    staleTime: Infinity, // Search results don't go stale
  });
}
```

### 3. Remove module-level cache variables
Delete cachedSections, cacheTimestamp, CACHE_TTL.

### 4. Simplify corpusSearch.ts
Keep only pure functions:
- loadAllSections() - no caching logic
- searchInSections(sections, query) - pure search

## Considerations

### Debouncing
Search queries should be debounced at the UI level, not here.
The QuoteBankViewer already has search input - ensure it debounces.

### Query Enabled State
Only run search query when:
- Sections are loaded
- Query is at least 2 characters

### Memory Usage
Query's gcTime will clean up old search results automatically.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Module-level cache variables removed
- [ ] useCorpusSections hook created
- [ ] useCorpusSearch hook created
- [ ] Search still works in QuoteBankViewer
- [ ] Memory usage doesn't grow unbounded

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3.4","title":"Task: Update role prompt templates to cite external evidence IDs","description":"# Goal
Ensure agents know how to reference external evidence records (EV-xxx) in a disciplined way.

---

# Why
Even if the schema/linter accept evidence refs, agents may not use them unless the prompts make the expectation explicit.

---

# Requirements
- Update prompt templates (kickoff role sections / specs) to:
  - prefer evidence refs (`EV-001#E1`) when making factual claims
  - keep `[inference]` labeling when no evidence is available
  - avoid “mystery citations”

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Role prompts mention evidence refs alongside Brenner transcript anchors (`§n`)
- [ ] Examples included (one per role)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","title":"Task: Pilot an evidence-pack-backed session on a non-code topic","description":"# Goal
Prove the external evidence channel is usable by running a session on a non-code topic where citations must come from an evidence pack (not model memory).

---

# Success criteria
- Choose a narrow question (one paragraph max).
- Build an evidence pack with at least:
  - 1 paper/preprint record
  - 1 dataset/benchmark record (or equivalent empirical source)
- Post evidence into the thread (so agents can read it).
- Kickoff instructs agents to cite evidence IDs/anchors (e.g., `EV-001#E1`).
- Compiled artifact contains explicit evidence references.

---

# Guardrails
- Keep the evidence excerpts short and clearly anchored.
- Distinguish “quoted/cited” vs `[inference]`.
- If a claim lacks evidence, it must be marked as hypothesis/inference.

---

# Output
- Thread id
- Evidence pack summary (IDs + provenance)
- Final compiled artifact demonstrating citations

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.2","title":"EPIC: Corpus Engine (parse/search/quote-bank/excerpts)","description":"# Goal
Make the corpus usable as a *tool*, not just a document:
- fast full-text search across the transcript + distillations
- stable anchors (`§n`) and excerpt composition
- a quote bank tagged by operators/primitives

# Why this matters
The corpus is the "ground truth". If it’s hard to search, cite, and excerpt, the whole system drifts into ungrounded synthesis.
